title: SIP-AI Signed Inference Receipt
description: An accountability artifact signed by a provider over a completed inference request. The signature covers the canonical form of every field except `signature` itself.

receipt_version.description: Schema/version identifier for this receipt format.

request_id.description: Opaque, client-generated request identifier. MUST NOT encode user identity.

provider_pubkey.description: Provider Ed25519 public key, base64url-encoded, prefixed with 'ed25519:'.

model_manifest_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the canonical model manifest the provider claims to have served.

runtime.description: Runtime engine the provider claims to have used.

runtime_version.description: Version string of the runtime engine (optional but recommended).

price_units.description: Settlement unit. 'pic' = Private Inference Credits; 'test' = non-settling test units.

price_amount.description: Amount charged, encoded as a decimal STRING to avoid float non-determinism in the signed payload.

privacy_mode.description: Privacy/transport mode used for this request.

started_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp when the provider accepted the request.

completed_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp when the provider finished the response.

response_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the canonical response body. Lets a client prove the response was not altered post-signing.

request_hash.description: Optional SHA-256 hash of the canonical request (model + messages). Lets a client prove the receipt corresponds to its request, e.g. when routing through an untrusted relay.

signature.description: Ed25519 signature (base64url) over the canonical receipt with the 'signature' field removed.
